source_jobs.title: Talent Partner
source_jobs.description:
About Us

Rho is the modern banking platform built for startups. Open accounts in minutes, issue cards, manage expenses, pay bills, and close the books – all in one connected platform backed by real human support.

About the Role

At Rho, we seek to attract, develop, and retain extraordinarily talented people. We believe that who we hire is central to what we can accomplish. As our Talent Partner, your job will be to uphold that exceptional standard and refine the infrastructure that enables us to hire great people.

In this role, you will play a key part in sourcing, attracting, and hiring top-tier talent for our Engineering and Operations divisions. You will also have the opportunity to assist with other roles as necessary, including those based in the United States and Europe.

As a partner to our business, you will uphold high recruiting standards and continuously work to enhance our hiring practices.

What You’ll Do

 - Own full-cycle recruiting for a defined set of roles - build relationships with the best talent in the tech ecosystem and find creative ways to connect with people.

 - Focus on quality over quantity: a lean team of A+ talent beats a large team of B-players, and that starts with your pipeline.

 - Partner closely with hiring managers on their searches - calibrate scorecards, advise on process, and keep searches moving.

 - Treat recruiting like half art, half science: find creative ways to position Rho with strong operators, A/B test your messaging, and take a human-first approach.

 - Track your metrics and own hitting them - time to close, pipeline health, and headcount goals.

 - Go where you're needed. Everyone at Rho goes beyond their job description - whether that's hiring for an Engineering role or walking a candidate to their interview room, this role is no exception.

 

What You Bring

 - 3 or more years of full-cycle recruiting experience, ideally with a technical emphasis

 - A passion for experimentation and craft: you multiply your leverage with AI and are always looking for a better way to work

 - Experience operating in high-accountability environments (startups, fintech, financial services, or similarly rigorous orgs).

 - High ownership and accountability—you take responsibility for getting the job done.

 - Knowledge of the talent market—where to find excellent builders, and how to speak their language.

 - Operational excellence—you know how to run a disciplined search.

 - Clear, persuasive communication and storytelling skills.

 - A bias toward action and a sense of ownership.

 - Experience tracking metrics and utilizing data to make decisions, including time to close and headcount goals

 - A track record of hitting or exceeding performance goals consistently by engaging and closing hires that move on to be successful in their roles

Diversity is a core value at Rho. We’re passionate about building and sustaining an inclusive and equitable environment for all those involved with our mission, including employees, contractors, candidates, customers and vendors. We believe every member of the Rho community enriches our ability to provide a broad range of ways to understand and engage with the market, identify problems, and drive solutions that align with our mission. We welcome all qualified applications and support each of our Rho’ers with ongoing professional growth opportunities.

source_jobs.title: Advisor, Startup Banking -  Austin
source_jobs.description:
About Us

Rho is the modern banking platform built for startups. Open accounts in minutes, issue cards, manage expenses, pay bills, and close the books – all in one connected platform backed by real human support.

About the Role

We're looking for an Advisor, Startup Banking to research, identify, and acquire new clients through self-generated leads and establishing active partnerships on a local and national level. You will work with Rho leaders and team members across the organization to deliver on our mission of making finance frictionless for organizations by promoting and implementing Rho’s differentiated technology to mid-market clients.

Responsibilities

 - Generate new business opportunities by identifying and prospecting potential middle-market businesses.

 - Build and maintain strong relationships with existing and prospective clients to maximize account retention and identify upselling or cross-selling opportunities.

 - Conduct market research and analysis to identify industry trends, competitive positioning, and potential target clients for effective business development strategies.

 - Collaborate with internal stakeholders across Product, Client Success, and Sales Development, to educate the team on banking products and align them with the specific needs and preferences of middle-market businesses.

 - Monitor and report on sales activities, market trends, and competitor activities, providing insights and recommendations for continuous improvement and competitive advantage.

 - Foster strong collaboration with internal teams to ensure a smooth and efficient sales cycle from initial client engagement through to deal closure and onboarding new clients, while ensuring client satisfaction and long-term relationship building.

 - Develop and deliver persuasive sales presentations, proposals, and product demonstrations to potential clients, highlighting the value proposition and benefits of the banking products offered.

 - Actively participate in networking events, conferences, and industry forums to expand professional networks, enhance the organization's visibility, and identify new business opportunities.

Qualifications

 - 2+ years of experience as a full-cycle sales professional; B2B sales and experience selling business banking/credit card products

 - B2B sales background with track record of selling to SMBs/Middle-Market (company revenues $20m+)

 - Posses deep local market network; experienced in cold calling and hunting new business

 - Understands how to sell into traditional and non-traditional companies

 - Experience working in complex sales processes involving multiple stakeholders that are part of the C-Suite

 - A passion for networking, building relationships, and being part of a team

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills

 - Proficient with sales tools including Salesforce and LinkedIn Sales Navigator

Our people are our most valuable asset. The budgeted base salary for this role is $83,000 - $115,000 with an OTE of $126,000 - $220,000. Base salary may vary depending on relevant experience, skills, and business needs. In addition to base pay, Rho offers equity, healthcare benefits and paid time off.

We’re passionate about building and sustaining an inclusive and equitable environment for all those involved with our mission, including employees, contractors, candidates, customers and vendors. We believe every member of the Rho community enriches our ability to provide a broad range of ways to understand and engage with the market, identify problems, and drive solutions that align with our mission. We welcome all qualified applications and support each of our Rho’ers with ongoing professional growth opportunities.

source_jobs.title: Associate Account Executive, San Francisco
source_jobs.description:
About Us

Rho is the modern banking platform built for startups. Open accounts in minutes, issue cards, manage expenses, pay bills, and close the books – all in one connected platform backed by real human support.

About the Role

As an Associate Account Executive at Rho, you will play a pivotal role in our sales team, focusing on lead generation, sales support, and client engagement. This position is designed to be a launching pad for your career in fintech and software sales, offering a clear path to becoming a full Account Executive within a year.

In this role, you will be at the forefront of Rho's growth initiatives, working closely with Senior Account Executives to drive new business and support existing client relationships. You'll have the opportunity to immerse yourself in the dynamic world of fintech, gaining hands-on experience with our innovative all-in-one financial platform that combines corporate cards, payments, and banking services.

Your responsibilities will span a range of critical sales activities, from conducting outreach through phone, email, and in-person interactions to attending industry events and meeting potential clients. This position will challenge you to develop a deep understanding of Rho's value proposition and articulate it effectively to prospects across various industries.

Key Responsibilities

 - Conduct lead-generation activities through phone, email, and in-person interactions.

 - Provide sales assistance and deal support to senior Account Executives

 - Attend industry events and meet with potential clients to build relationships

 - Learn and articulate Rho's value proposition to prospects

 - Maintain accurate records in our CRM system

 - Collaborate with marketing and product teams to stay updated on new features and promotions.

 - Contribute to the development of sales strategies and tactics.

Requirements

 - Bachelor's degree in Business, Finance, Marketing, or a related field.

 - Excellent communication, interpersonal, and presentation skills.

 - Strong organizational and time management abilities.

 - Ability to work in a fast-paced, dynamic environment

 - Passion for fintech and willingness to learn about financial products and services

Our people are our most valuable asset. The base salary for this role is $70,500. In addition to base pay, Rho offers equity, healthcare benefits and paid time off. This role is eligible for an uncapped performance bonus paid quarterly. Inclusive of on-target performance bonuses, the total salary range for this role is $90,500.

source_jobs.title: Staff Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
About Us

Rho is the modern banking platform built for startups. Open accounts in minutes, issue cards, manage expenses, pay bills, and close the books – all in one connected platform backed by real human support.

About the Role

As a Staff Product Designer at Rho, you will champion the craft of design to deliver experiences that are not only functional but thoughtfully refined. You’ll take end-to-end ownership of complex product areas, consistently holding a high bar for usability, visual polish, and quality of execution. With an instinct for user behavior and a relentless attention to detail, you'll bring clarity to ambiguous problems and advocate for the end user in every decision. Working closely with product and engineering partners, you'll lead the creation of intuitive solutions that feel seamless, scalable, and rooted in empathy—ensuring every design moment reflects Rho's commitment to frictionless finance.

Responsibilities

 - Lead design efforts for end-to-end product features across multiple surfaces

 - Own complex design problems with minimal oversight—from exploration to polished execution.

 - Collaborate deeply with product managers and engineers to shape scope, identify dependencies, and optimize user flows.

 - Define interaction patterns and craft scalable, accessible solutions informed by data and user research.

 - Develop prototypes to test concepts, gather feedback, and drive alignment.

 - Uphold and evolve design quality by considering both micro-interactions and holistic experience.

 - Communicate ideas clearly across design critiques, cross-functional meetings, and stakeholder reviews.

 - Contribute to design system enhancements and advocate for design quality across teams.



Qualifications

 - 6+ years of experience designing digital products, with a portfolio demonstrating end-to-end ownership. Fintech experience preferred but is not necessary

 - Skilled in prototyping, UI/UX, interaction design, and visual polish.

 - Proven ability to work autonomously, plan work, and push projects forward independently.

 - Comfortable designing for ambiguity, resolving complex workflows, and simplifying edge cases.

 - Strong collaboration and communication skills; experience presenting to executives and external partners.

 - Familiar with applying qualitative and quantitative research methods to inform decisions.

 - Experience working with or contributing to a design system.

 - Adaptable, pragmatic, and empathetic—able to thrive in fast-changing, collaborative environments

Our people are our most valuable asset. The salary range for this role is $195,000-$260,000. Base salary may vary depending on relevant experience, skills, and business needs. In addition to base pay, Rho offers equity, healthcare benefits, and paid time off.

source_jobs.description:
About Us

Rho is the modern banking platform built for startups. Open accounts in minutes, issue cards, manage expenses, pay bills, and close the books – all in one connected platform backed by real human support.

About the Role

We're looking for an expert Account Manager to manage a portfolio of our largest B2B relationships. You will work with your peers on the Customer Success team and your Manager to develop and execute repeatable tactics for onboarding and managing ongoing relationships with the businesses in your portfolio. Your north star is to drive Rho product adoption for your clients at scale, allowing you to grow your customers' revenue and maintain high retention. In this role, you will work highly collaboratively with your teammates on the Success team and cross-functionally with the Product and Customer Service teams.

Responsibilities

 - Manage, retain, and expand existing client relationships in our middle-market, VC-backed start ups, and commercial segments.

 - Strategically collaborate with the Account Executive Team (Sales) to efficiently and effectively hand off and onboard new clients.

 - Build and nurture relationships with key accounts to understand and address their business needs, priorities, goals, and challenges to maximize client adoption of Rho’s entire product suite and services.

 - Guide senior customer stakeholders through the changes needed to unlock Rho's full value, providing guidance to help them design the optimal enablement and adoption strategy to maximize their business value.

 - Track and report key account metrics, such as revenue growth, retention, and customer satisfaction.

 - Identify upsell opportunities and deliver consultative value.

Key attributes

 - Self-motivated with impeccable interpersonal and communication skills.

 - Bias for action, enthusiasm about technology and fintech, comfort with ambiguity, and high speed.

 - A high degree of comfort with drawing insights from analytics reports.

 - A valued team member who upholds Rho’s mission and professional values.

Qualifications

 - 2+ years of professional experience in a client-facing Customer Success role

 - Has experience managing large-scale implementations with 50+ users

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills

 - A track record of managing a large portfolio of clients efficiently and at scale

 - Prior experience working in B2B SaaS, high-growth preferable

 - BA degree or higher

 - Prior background in accounting, finance, cards, or payment is preferred

Our people are our most valuable asset. This role is based out of our San Francisco or NYC office, with an $125,000 to $145,000. source_jobs.title: Design Technologist
source_jobs.description:
About Us

Rho is the modern banking platform built for startups. Open accounts in minutes, issue cards, manage expenses, pay bills, and close the books – all in one connected platform backed by real human support.


THE ROLE

Rho is hiring Design Technologists — people who design products by building them. You lead with taste and user intuition, but you work in the medium: prototyping in code, leveraging AI tools, getting to real signal before anyone's written a PRD. You're not waiting for requirements. You're generating them.

The design role has more leverage than it's ever had. AI is compressing the space between insight and artifact. A designer who can prototype in code, think in systems, and build deep customer intuition can now shape product in ways that weren't possible two years ago. This role exists to capture that.

At Rho, you'll own product problems from first principles through shipped software. You'll be the person in the room who knows the customer cold, has the design judgment to know what's right, and has the technical fluency to prove it fast. This role is for someone who's already working this way — and wants a company that's building around it.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Design by making. Own complex product areas end-to-end. Use functional prototypes — not decks — as your primary tool for thinking, aligning, and deciding. Work in high fidelity early. Ship the concept, not just the comp.

Know the customer. Build real expertise in how founders and early-stage companies manage money. Use research when it's right, but also trust the pattern recognition that comes from sustained proximity to the problem.

Set product direction. Your design judgment is a strategic input, not a downstream service. Shape what gets built, what gets cut, and what gets rethought. Bring clarity to ambiguity. Say no to the right things.

Hold an unreasonable standard for craft. Usability, interaction quality, visual refinement — every detail in a banking product earns or erodes trust. Sweat accordingly.

Work AI-native. Use AI tools to accelerate your own process. Identify where AI belongs in the product experience itself. Help the team build new workflows as the old ones stop making sense.

Push the system forward. Evolve Rho's design system — not just components, but the principles that make a complex financial platform feel simple.




WHO YOU ARE

 - 4+ years designing digital products. A portfolio that shows exceptional craft and end-to-end ownership — not just the pretty screens, but evidence that you shaped the outcome. Fintech is a plus, not a gate.

 - You prototype beyond Figma — in code, with AI tools, in whatever gets you to a better answer faster. You're not an engineer, but you're not afraid of the material.

 - You work autonomously and thrive in ambiguity. You plan your own work. You don't need to be managed toward quality.

 - You design for real complexity — edge cases, dense workflows, the unglamorous stuff that separates good products from great ones.

 - You communicate with precision and know how to tell the story of why, not just what. You can hold your own with executives, engineers, and end users.

 - You have taste. When you say something isn't good enough, people believe you.



Our people are our most valuable asset. The salary range for this role is $130,000 - $280,000. Base salary may vary depending on relevant experience, skills, and business needs. In addition to base pay, Rho offers equity, healthcare benefits, and paid time off.

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Us

Rho is the modern banking platform built for startups. Open accounts in minutes, issue cards, manage expenses, pay bills, and close the books – all in one connected platform backed by real human support.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Software Engineer at Rho, you'll build the financial infrastructure powering the next generation of founders. This isn't a role where you sit inside a lane. You'll move across the stack, own problems end-to-end, and talk directly to the customers using what you ship. We're hiring engineers who are restless about impact, reach for AI tools the way other people reach for documentation, and genuinely want to understand why what they're building matters.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own problems from scoping to production. Write the code, ship it, watch it land.

 - Work across the stack. You go where the problem is, not where your comfort zone ends.

 - Talk to customers. Sit in on calls, read support tickets, watch session replays. Build what people actually need.

 - Use AI in your daily workflow. Code generation, debugging, spec drafting, and spot where it changes what's possible.

 - Move fast and iterate in the open. Get something in front of users, then make it better.

 - Pick up what's falling. If something's broken, fix it.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 5+ years of software engineering experience in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.

 - Genuine full-stack flexibility. You've shipped across the stack and don't treat the backend/frontend divide as a hard boundary.

 - Genuine AI fluency. You use these tools every day and think about where they raise the ceiling on what you can build.

 - A bias to ship. You'd rather get something in front of a user than perfect it in a doc.

 - Customer curiosity. You want to know the person on the other side of what you're building, and you'll go find out.

 - High agency. You make progress when things are unclear, surface problems early, and own your outcomes.




COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

Our people are our most valuable asset. The salary range for this role is $170,000 - $270,000. In addition to base pay, Rho offers equity, healthcare benefits and paid time off.

Diversity, empathy and transparency are core values at Rho. We are passionate about building and sustaining an inclusive and equitable environment for all those involved with our mission including employees, contractors, candidates, customers and vendors. We believe every member of the Rho community enriches our ability to provide a broad range of ways to understand and engage with the market, identify problems and drive solutions that align with our mission of making finance frictionless for business.

source_jobs.title: VP of Product
source_jobs.description:
About Us

Rho is the modern banking platform built for startups. Open accounts in minutes, issue cards, manage expenses, pay bills, and close the books – all in one connected platform backed by real human support.




THE ROLE

Rho is hiring a VP of Product. Reporting into our CEO, you’ll partner with our design and engineering teams to set Rho's product direction and own its execution. You'll lead a product team, but you'll also be in the weeds: close enough to the work and close enough to the customer that your judgment is earned, not inherited from a dashboard. You'll know what founders need before they ask for it, because you've built the intuition that comes from proximity, not process.

How products get built is fundamentally changing. The line between thinking about something and making it real is collapsing. The best product leaders right now aren't just setting strategy and reviewing work. They're in the build, shaping the product with their hands, using every tool available to move faster and see further.

Rho is rebuilding the finance stack for the innovation economy. What happens next depends on the quality of decisions we make and the speed at which we make them. An exceptional product team gets us there.





WHAT YOU'LL DO

Shape product direction. Own the product vision alongside the CEO and executive team. Translate Rho's company strategy into a product roadmap that's opinionated, well-sequenced, and grounded in what actually matters to customers. Know when to go deep on one bet or test many.

Lead and grow a team of 6 PMs. Enable Rho’s PM function with agency, a spirit of experimentation, and a one-team culture.

Build for founders. Develop a deep, almost unfair understanding of how founders and early-stage companies think about money, time, and risk. See around corners. Design the product for the workflow they don't know they want yet — the desire path, not just the paved one.

Get in the weeds. This isn't a role where you review mocks on Fridays. You're prototyping, pressure-testing, iterating alongside design and engineering. You have opinions about the details. AI is changing what it means to be in the weeds, and you're leaning into that.

Set the tempo. Create pace and urgency without chaos. Define what "fast" means for Rho's product org— how decisions get made, how work moves through the system, how the team stays focused on what matters. Remove friction. Raise the clock speed.

Win with us. This is a company-building role, not just a product role. You'll contribute to decisions that extend well beyond your function— go-to-market, hiring, company strategy. You care about Rho winning, not just Product winning.

Enable the team. Build and lead a product team that punches above its weight. Create clarity so people can move fast. Hire well. Develop strong product thinkers. Make the people around you better at their jobs.






WHO YOU ARE

You’ve launched 0 to 1 product builds that had meaningful customer impact. You obsess over the details that make something exceptional, and you iterate towards perfect.

You've led product at a high-growth startup and you've shipped products that customers love.

You build. You're not above opening a prototype, sketching a flow, or writing a spec yourself. You understand that AI is changing the product development lifecycle, and you're actively working that way, not theorizing about it.

You have deep empathy for founders and the chaos of early-stage companies. You've either been one or spent enough time with them to think like one.

You communicate with clarity and conviction. You can align a room, make a hard call, and bring people along — even when the answer is no.

You have taste. You care about craft. You know that in a banking product, every interaction carries trust, and you hold the team to that standard.

About Us

Rho is the modern banking platform built for startups. Open accounts in minutes, issue cards, manage expenses, pay bills, and close the books – all in one connected platform backed by real human support.


THE ROLE

Rho is hiring a Growth Marketing Lead to own acquisition end-to-end across both paid and organic channels, bringing more tech-forward businesses onto Rho. You'll work directly with the VP of Marketing, and you'll partner with and mentor our Growth Associate across both acquisition and lifecycle.

This is a builder's seat with real ownership. You'll set the creative and channel strategy, move fast, test relentlessly, and put real budget behind what works. If you do your best work when you own the outcome end to end, read on.

This is an IC role for someone with 5+ years of experience who has owned both paid and organic channels and a number, moved fast without sacrificing rigor, and has genuine fluency with AI as part of how they work - not as a tool they're learning, but as a tool that already changes what they can ship in a week.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Launch and scale growth channels end to end, paid and organic: paid social, YouTube, organic social, and influencer and affiliate plays. You own strategy, targeting, creative direction, testing, and optimization.

 - Treat creative as the growth lever. Performance is a creative and targeting problem before it's a budget problem. You'll run high-velocity creative testing, read the signal fast, and pour fuel on what works.

 - Build the playbook for acquiring tech-forward small businesses at scale: find the channels and creative that reach them efficiently, prove the model, and scale what works segment by segment.

 - Own a number. You'll carry CAC, LTV, and payback targets, decompose them, run bets against them, and stay honest about what's working and what isn't.

 - Partner with and mentor our Growth Associate, including close collaboration on lifecycle marketing - onboarding, activation, and retention - so paid acquisition and lifecycle reinforce each other rather than running on separate tracks.

 - Operate as a true partner to sales. Over-communicate, share what's converting, and treat the pods as collaborators in the funnel, not a handoff.

 - Build attribution you can trust. Where the platforms fall short, get scrappy about measuring what's actually driving pipeline.

 - Use AI to do things that weren't possible before: generating and iterating ad concepts at speed, building testing and reporting workflows that compress your cycle time, and exploring builds that blur the line between marketer and agentic operator. You're not waiting for a tutorial - you're figuring it out.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 4-6+ years in growth or performance marketing with hands-on ownership of paid and organic channels and a budget you were accountable for.

 - A track record of owning a number and hitting it. You can walk us through a goal, how you broke it down, the bets you ran, and what actually happened, including the ones that didn't work.

 - Strong creative instincts. You can brief, evaluate, and iterate on ad creative, especially video, and you have a real point of view on what makes a hook land. A weak hook registers before the first second is up.

 - Genuine scrappiness: examples of fast-to-launch programs that drove real pipeline, not polished campaigns that quietly underperformed.

 - Full-funnel fluency, from awareness to conversion, with the judgment to fix the bottleneck that actually matters before scaling spend.

 - CAC discipline. You treat the budget like it's your own money and can defend every dollar.

 - Genuine fluency with AI tools - already active in your workflow, not aspirational.

 - High agency and low ego. You don't wait to be told what to do, and you're honest about what hasn't worked.

 - You communicate with clarity: you show up with context, bring stakeholders along early, and tie decisions to outcomes.




BONUS POINTS

 - Experience

source_jobs.title: Senior Marketing Manager, LA
source_jobs.description:
About Us

Rho is the modern banking platform built for startups. Open accounts in minutes, issue cards, manage expenses, pay bills, and close the books – all in one connected platform backed by real human support.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're hiring a Senior Marketing Manager to own Rho's presence in Los Angeles: community, partnerships, brand, demand, and customer growth. You'll own a pipeline number, a real budget, and a tight partnership with our local sales rep. You'll be on the ground - meeting with investors, visiting customer offices, showing up where it matters.

LA's founder ecosystem is one of the most diverse in the country. Defense and aerospace alongside consumer brands, media and entertainment, biotech, and a fast-growing tech layer underneath all of it. The founders building here span industries, backgrounds, and funding sources in ways that don't look like any other market. That's what makes it interesting - and what makes generic approaches fail.

We have early traction. Now time to help us grow it.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the pipeline number. Sourced pipeline, startup customers, attribution in Salesforce. You set the targets and drive them through every motion you run.

 - Grow our LA customer count. Identify and close expansion opportunities within our existing base, run in-person touches with high-value accounts, and host customer-only gatherings that deepen relationships and surface new ones.

 - Partner with our LA sales rep. You generate demand and build relationships; together you close. Tight collaboration on targeting, follow-up, and account coverage.

 - Get out in the market. Meet with VCs, family offices, and accelerators. Visit customer offices. Show up at the right events. This is not a desk job.

 - Build founder community across sectors. Host the events founders actually want to attend - dinners and roundtables that bring together builders from defense, consumer, media, and tech. Curate every guest list. Become a connector across LA's distinct founder communities.

 - Own vertical relationships. Build direct relationships with the accelerators, incubators, studio programs, and industry networks that feed LA's founder pipeline - from Techstars and LA Cleantech Incubator to entertainment and defense ecosystem programs.

 - Own how Rho shows up in market. Sponsorships, local press, activations, founder content. Partner with our brand and creative team to execute.

 - Run local growth experiments. Geo-targeted paid, branded search, ABM into top LA accounts, lifecycle nurture tailored to LA founders.

 - Define your own approach. No established playbook. You decide what works, run it, and document what you learn.


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 6+ years of experience across marketing, community, partnerships, brand, or growth at a venture-backed company. B2B SaaS, fintech, defense tech, consumer brands, or media are all strong fits.

 - High agency. You don't wait to be told what to do. You form a point of view, act on it, and adjust. The VP of Marketing can be a thought partner, but can't be your operating system.

 - A track record of growing something - a business, a product line, a market, a community. You can point to before and after and explain what you did to move it.

 - Genuinely comfortable being out in the market. Meeting with investors, visiting customer offices, building relationships face to face. You'd rather be in a founder's office than on a Zoom.

 - Sociable and naturally good with people. Event marketing is a core part of how Rho builds in market, and you're the kind of person founders actually want to spend time with.

 - Comfortable working across industries. LA's founder base is not monolithic. You can move between a defense tech founder, a CPG operator, and a media entrepreneur and find the right footing in each conversation.

 - Comfortable with data and finance. You can read a pipeline dashboard, run a budget, interpret a growth experiment, and talk credibly with finance-mind

source_jobs.title: Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Us

Rho is the modern banking platform built for startups. Open accounts in minutes, issue cards, manage expenses, pay bills, and close the books – all in one connected platform backed by real human support.


ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Product Manager at Rho, you'll help build the financial platform powering the next generation of founders — and rethink how the innovation economy banks. You'll own a meaningful surface end-to-end, get close to the customers depending on it, and ship product that makes their companies easier to run. We're hiring PMs who hold their own product judgment as a craft, partner deeply across the organization, and operate on the edge of what's just now possible.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own a customer job-to-be-done end-to-end — from understanding the problem deeply through shipping, iterating, and seeing it land.

 - Build conviction through discovery. Frame the problem, sharpen the hypothesis, and define what success looks like before you build.

 - Decide what matters most. Make clear, deliberate calls about what to do now, what to do next, and what to leave behind.

 - Partner deeply across the org — Engineering, Design, Operations, GTM, and leadership — and bring people along on the why.

 - Do what's just now possible. Bring AI and emerging capabilities into how you work, and look for places they unlock step-change product improvements.

 - Be a catalyst for velocity. The surfaces you touch ship faster, learn faster, and get better because you're on them.




QUALIFICATIONS

 - 3+ years of product management or equivalent experience in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.

 - Strong product taste. You hold your own view on what good looks like and ship work you're proud to put your name on.

 - Discovery rigor. You can articulate the problem, the hypothesis, and how you'll know it worked before you've written a spec.

 - Frontier fluency. You actively use emerging tools to change what's possible in how you work and what you build.

 - Cross-functional partnership. You build trust quickly across the org, communicate proactively, and bring people along on the why.

 - High agency. You move in ambiguity, make calls without a playbook, and own the outcome.

 - A craving for the kind of team that pushes you. Smart, creative, ambitious people to build alongside who make the work fun and help you grow faster than you thought possible.

 

Our people are our most valuable asset. The salary range for this role is $165,000 - $285,000. In addition to base pay, Rho offers equity, healthcare benefits and paid time off.

Diversity, empathy and transparency are core values at Rho. We are passionate about building and sustaining an inclusive and equitable environment for all those involved with our mission including employees, contractors, candidates, customers and vendors. We believe every member of the Rho community enriches our ability to provide a broad range of ways to understand and engage with the market, identify problems and drive solutions that align with our mission of making finance frictionless for business.

source_jobs.title: Founding Account Executive, Los Angeles
source_jobs.description:
About Us

Rho is the modern banking platform built for startups. Open accounts in minutes, issue cards, manage expenses, pay bills, and close the books – all in one connected platform backed by real human support.

About the Role

We’re seeking a Los Angeles sales professional with a track record of success in achieving new business and growing a current book of business. The ideal candidate will be driven, organized, and display a natural ability to build rapport and trust. We’re looking for that perfect blend of sales drive coupled with a customer-first mentality that will help us create loyal, happy customers.

Rho has built a substantial community in the New York startup market. Hosting community-driven events, curated get-togethers, and more for founders and teams in the ecosystem has spread the Rho brand across many verticals. Los Angeles will be growing at the same speed and the Rho team with it. You will be responsible for organizing and attending Rho-sponsored events throughout the market geared towards building community and onboarding new business.

Responsibilities

 - Event-Driven Role – Attend multiple in-person networking events every week to build relationships and engage with the startup community. Must be comfortable with frequent face-to-face interactions.

 - Identify and target high-growth startups in the Los Angeles area for Rho’s cash management platform.

 - Win new business in competitive sales against the industry’s incumbents and newer players.

 - Attend Rho-sponsored events in person to build relationships and community.

 - Develop and execute sales strategies to meet and exceed revenue targets.

 - Ramp relationships and expand referral volume through networking.

 - Advocate for client needs to inform Rho’s roadmap.

 - Win new business in competitive sales against the industry’s incumbents and newer players.

 - Construct, forecast, and manage your sales pipeline to meet company goals.

 - Deliver comprehensive product demos and answer technical inquiries.

 - Coordinate closely with customer success and implementation teams to maintain the highest level of service and experience.



Qualifications

 - 3+ years of professional experience in a closing sales role.

 - 1+ years of experience managing a book of business and carrying a quota, focusing on net-new acquisitions.

 - Previous experience working in any one of the following areas is preferred:
   
   - Experience selling subscription-based software technologies (SaaS), other software products, and/or financial services products.
   
   - Experience working with startups.

 - Top-performer with a track record of consistently exceeding quota.

 - A consummate team player who enjoys wearing multiple hats and inspiring everyone around you to do great work.

 - A builder who is excited to help shape the foundation for Rhos’ Startup Banking team in Los Angeles.

Nice-to-Have

 - Familiarity with the startup ecosystem and venture accelerators

Our people are our most valuable asset. In addition to base pay, Rho offers equity, healthcare benefits and paid time off.

Diversity is a core value at Rho. We’re passionate about building and sustaining an inclusive and equitable environment for all those involved with our mission, including employees, contractors, candidates, customers and vendors. We believe every member of the Rho community enriches our ability to provide a broad range of ways to understand and engage with the market, identify problems, and drive solutions that align with our mission. We welcome all qualified applications and support each of our Rho’ers with ongoing professional growth opportunities.

source_jobs.title: Accelerator Partnerships Lead
source_jobs.description:
About Us

Rho is the modern banking platform built for startups. Open accounts in minutes, issue cards, manage expenses, pay bills, and close the books – all in one connected platform backed by real human support.



ABOUT THE ROLE

This is not a general accelerator partnerships role. You will own Rho's relationships with the programs that define startup culture in your market—whether that's the most selective, high-signal programs in the world or the community-driven ecosystem that produces the next generation of high-growth founders. Your job is to make Rho the default financial platform they adopt from day one.

These markets are relationship-dense. You will succeed not by running a standard partnerships playbook, but by becoming a trusted operator in your ecosystem—someone who shows up before there is an ask. Success is not measured by activity. It is measured by whether founders in these programs choose Rho before Demo Day.

We are hiring one person for this role in New York City and one in San Francisco.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN


PROGRAM RELATIONSHIPS

 - Own the primary relationship with Managing Directors, Group Partners, and program leads at the highest-signal accelerators in your market—you are the first call they make when thinking about a financial platform for their founders.

 - Build trusted relationships at the staff level (partners, associates, community teams) so Rho has visibility into cohort dynamics and emerging needs.

 - Develop deep relationships across the broader VC community in your geography—including family offices, sector-specific funds, and university programs that feed the accelerator pipeline.

 - Represent Rho at the events, demo days, partner dinners, and community gatherings that define startup culture in your market. You will be a known, credible presence in these rooms.


FOUNDER ACQUISITION & ACTIVATION

 - Drive Rho adoption within each cohort from first conversation through activation, with clear conversion targets and a structured follow-up cadence.

 - Build and run founder-facing programming—office hours, workshops, fireside chats, roundtables—that delivers genuine value, not marketing disguised as content.

 - Partner with Sales on warm handoffs for high-ACV opportunities that emerge from the accelerator ecosystem.

 - Collaborate with Marketing to create scalable pipelines that move founders from a first impression to a fully activated client and referral partner.


PLAYBOOK & STRATEGY

 - Build and own the accelerator partnerships playbook for your market—from first contact through multi-cohort expansion and renewal—with clear KPIs: cohort penetration rate, activation velocity, and GP/ACV influenced.

 - Map and prioritize the accelerator landscape in your market; quality of cohort and alignment with Rho's ICP matter more than volume.

 - Evaluate new programs for inclusion and manage the portfolio with intentionality, not opportunism.

 - Feed founder insights from your cohorts back to Product and Marketing to inform roadmap decisions and targeted campaigns.


CROSS-FUNCTIONAL LEVERAGE

 - Partner with the VC Partnerships team to align accelerator strategy with LP and GP relationship development across your market.

 - Collaborate with Marketing on co-branded activations, founder panels, field events, and founder-facing content tied to program timelines.

 - Contribute to CRM hygiene and pipeline tracking so accelerator-sourced deals are visible, attributable, and reported with precision.




ABOUT YOU

 - You are embedded in the startup ecosystem in your target market—you know the accelerators, the programs, the operators, and the MDs, and they know you. You have direct, existing relationships inside the top programs in your geo; not peripheral familiarity, but real credibility with the people who run them.

 - You have 2–4 years of experience in VC partnerships, channel partnerships, or founder-facing GTM roles at a high-growth startup or venture-backed company—or you are a founder

source_jobs.title: Lead Talent Partner, Technology
source_jobs.description:
About Us

Rho is the modern banking platform built for startups. Open accounts in minutes, issue cards, manage expenses, pay bills, and close the books – all in one connected platform backed by real human support.



About the Role

At Rho, we seek to attract, develop and retain extraordinarily talented people. We believe that who we hire is central to what we can accomplish. As our Lead Talent Partner, your job will be to uphold that exceptional standard and refine the infrastructure that enables us to hire great people.

You will own your funnels end-to-end, improve our Talent operating rhythm, and leverage AI as a force-multiplier. Our People function has already made significant inroads embedding AI into their workflows and we empower every member of the team to be a builder.

This role reports directly to Rho’s Head of People & Talent and is based out of our NYC office, 5 days a week.



What You’ll Do

Build relationships with the best talent in the tech ecosystem and find creative ways to connect with people.

Focus on quality over quantity. We know that a lean team of A+ talent is infinitely better than a large team of B-players.

Strengthen our talent brand: Partner with marketing to run events, brainstorm

Develop rigorous hiring processes across the business and advise hiring teams on building better searches.

Treat recruiting like half art, half science: Find creative ways to position Rho before the strongest operators in the business; A/B test messaging; take a human-first approach.

Obsess over our metrics and beating them, all the time.

Go where you are needed. Everyone at Rho goes beyond their job description - whether it's hiring for a G&A role or guiding someone to their interview room, this role is no exception.



What You Bring

 - 5 or more years of full-cycle recruiting experience, ideally with a technical emphasis

 - A passion for experimentation and craft: you multiply your leverage with AI, try new tools, and refuse to stop until you get it right.

 - Experience operating in high-accountability environments (startups, fintech, financial services, or similarly rigorous orgs).

 - High ownership and accountability—you take responsibility for getting the job done.

 - Strong knowledge of the talent market—where to find excellent builders, and how to speak their language.

 - Operational excellence—you know how to run a disciplined search and coach hiring managers along the way.

 - Clear, persuasive communication and storytelling skills.

 - A bias toward action and sense of ownership.



Our people are our most valuable asset. Our target compensation range for this role is:

 - NYC: $150,000 - $195,000

 - Salt Lake City: $120,000 - $156,000

with variation based on relevant experience, skills, and business needs. In addition to base pay, Rho offers equity, comprehensive healthcare benefits, and paid time off.

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
About Us

Rho is the modern banking platform built for startups. Open accounts in minutes, issue cards, manage expenses, pay bills, and close the books – all in one connected platform backed by real human support.

About the Role

As a Senior Product Designer at Rho, you will champion the craft of design to deliver experiences that are not only functional but thoughtfully refined. You’ll take end-to-end ownership of complex product areas, consistently holding a high bar for usability, visual polish, and quality of execution. With an instinct for user behavior and a relentless attention to detail, you'll bring clarity to ambiguous problems and advocate for the end user in every decision. Working closely with product and engineering partners, you'll lead the creation of intuitive solutions that feel seamless, scalable, and rooted in empathy—ensuring every design moment reflects Rho's commitment to frictionless finance.

Responsibilities

 - Lead design efforts for end-to-end product features across multiple surfaces

 - Own complex design problems with minimal oversight—from exploration to polished execution.

 - Collaborate deeply with product managers and engineers to shape scope, identify dependencies, and optimize user flows.

 - Define interaction patterns and craft scalable, accessible solutions informed by data and user research.

 - Develop prototypes to test concepts, gather feedback, and drive alignment.

 - Uphold and evolve design quality by considering both micro-interactions and holistic experience.

 - Communicate ideas clearly across design critiques, cross-functional meetings, and stakeholder reviews.

 - Contribute to design system enhancements and advocate for design quality across teams.



Qualifications

 - 4+ years of experience designing digital products, with a portfolio demonstrating end-to-end ownership. Fintech experience preferred but is not necessary

 - Skilled in prototyping, UI/UX, interaction design, and visual polish.

 - Proven ability to work autonomously, plan work, and push projects forward independently.

 - Comfortable designing for ambiguity, resolving complex workflows, and simplifying edge cases.

 - Strong collaboration and communication skills; experience presenting to executives and external partners.

 - Familiar with applying qualitative and quantitative research methods to inform decisions.

 - Experience working with or contributing to a design system.

 - Adaptable, pragmatic, and empathetic—able to thrive in fast-changing, collaborative environments

Our people are our most valuable asset. The salary range for this role is $160,000 - $220,000. Base salary may vary depending on relevant experience, skills, and business needs. In addition to base pay, Rho offers equity, healthcare benefits, and paid time off.

Diversity is a core value at Rho. We’re passionate about building and sustaining an inclusive and equitable environment for all those involved with our mission, including employees, contractors, candidates, customers, and vendors. We believe every member of the Rho community enriches our ability to provide a broad range of ways to understand and engage with the market, identify problems, and drive solutions that align with our mission. We welcome all qualified applications and support each of our Rho’ers with ongoing professional growth opportunities.

notes: Rho is a fintech company with a mix of tech and non-tech roles. The company has a strong focus on design and product development.
